question: What is the definition of the acronym DHRS?

nuscale_ch01_0450.title: BTP 10-2, Rev 4: Design Guidelines for Avoiding Water Hammers in Steam GeneratorsTFSGD B.1 thru B.4Top-Feed Steam Generator Designs
nuscale_ch01_0450.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design does not use top-feed, preheat, or once-through steam generator designs, so the specific regulatory guidelines for avoiding water hammers in these systems are not applicable. Instead, NuScale uses a passive natural circulation system (DHRS) that fulfills a similar safety function without the need for auxiliary feedwater pumps or the design features addressed by these guidelines.

nuscale_ch01_0669.title: DSRS 15.1.1-15.1.4, Rev 0: Decrease in Feedwater Temperature, Increase in Feedwater Flow, Increase in Steam Flow, and Inadvertent Opening of the Turbine Bypass System or Inadvertent Operation of the Decay Heat Removal SystemII.4 Specific CriterionInstrument Spans and Setpoints use RG 1.105
nuscale_ch01_0669.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for abnormal operating occurrences such as changes in feedwater temperature, flow, steam flow, and inadvertent system operations. It confirms that system pressure, critical heat flux, and event classification criteria are met, and that instrument spans and setpoints comply with Regulatory Guide 1.105. Overall, NuScale’s analysis demonstrates alignment with the Standard Review Plan and Design Specific Review Standard requirements for these events.

nuscale_ch01_0043.title: Ultimate Heat Sink
nuscale_ch01_0043.summary: The Ultimate Heat Sink for the NuScale plant is a large, stainless steel-lined, reinforced concrete pool located below grade, comprising the reactor, refueling, and spent fuel pool areas. During normal operations, heat is removed via a cooling system and external heat sink, while in a loss of all AC power, decay heat is passively transferred to the pool, which has sufficient water inventory to ensure continued cooling through heat up and boiling.

nuscale_ch01_0673.title: DSRS 15.1.1-15.1.4, Rev 0: Decrease in Feedwater Temperature, Increase in Feedwater Flow, Increase in Steam Flow, and Inadvertent Opening of the Turbine Bypass System or Inadvertent Operation of the Decay Heat Removal SystemII.3 Analytical ParametersCore Burnup
nuscale_ch01_0673.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for DSRS 15.1.1-15.1.4, addressing events such as decrease in feedwater temperature, increase in feedwater or steam flow, and inadvertent operation of turbine bypass or decay heat removal systems. It confirms that analytical parameters—including limiting single failure, initial power level, conservative scram, core burnup, and setpoint inaccuracies—meet regulatory requirements, with no exceptions noted.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. Tables and figures are numbered according to their section, and sensitive content is withheld from the public version in compliance with regulatory requirements.

edges.description: Section A states that the NuScale DHRS fulfills a similar function as the AFW system in large PWRs, while Section B references the Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) in the context of system operations and safety analysis, connecting both sections through the functional role of DHRS.

Section A explains that the NuScale DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) serves a similar function to the Auxiliary Feedwater System in large PWRs, while Section B describes the DHRS as a system that removes decay heat and provides cooling to the reactor coolant system, thus both sections connect by describing the function and operational context of the DHRS.
